activities_projects.title: Mapping the Global Textile Flow
activities_projects.objective: Visualize the trade link between Manchester and Indian industrial hubs
steps: Locate Manchester and Liverpool in Britain
Identify and mark Mumbai, Ahmedabad, and Kanpur on the India map
Draw arrows to show the export of raw cotton from India and the import of finished cloth from Britain
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the geographic and economic interdependence in a colonial economy
activities_projects.evaluation: Geographic accuracy and clarity of trade flow (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Gomastha Conflict Storyboard
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the impact of the Company's direct control over weavers
steps: Create a 4-panel storyboard: 1. Appointment of Gomastha, 2. Giving advances to weavers, 3. Inspection of quality, 4. Clash between Gomastha and weavers
Write captions explaining why weavers lost their bargaining power
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the shift from independent weavers to supervised laborers
activities_projects.evaluation: Historical narrative accuracy and thematic depth (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Industrial Advertisements: A Cultural Audit
activities_projects.objective: Examine how manufacturers used religious icons to sell goods
steps: Find or draw labels that used images of Indian Gods (e.g., Krishna or Lakshmi)
Explain how these images were intended to make foreign products feel familiar
Design your own label for a product using a 'nationalist' symbol to attract buyers
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understanding marketing strategies and cultural imperialism
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical thinking and creative application (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Jobber's Role-Play
activities_projects.objective: Explore the recruitment process in early Indian factories
steps: Assign roles: An old, trusted worker (The Jobber), a hopeful migrant, and a Mill owner
Act out the Jobber bringing a worker from his village and providing him life-essentials for a price
Discuss the Jobber’s power over the lives of factory workers
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Insights into the informal authority structures in industrial India
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of the Jobber's role and socio-economic context (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Steam Engine vs. Hand Labour Matrix
activities_projects.objective: Understand why machines were adopted slowly in some industries
steps: List industries that preferred machines (standardized products) in one circle
List industries that preferred hand labor (intricate designs, seasonal work) in the other
Identify common factors like the cost of repair and labor abundance in the intersection
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Grasps the economic logic behind technological transition
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical classification and depth of reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Proto-Industrialisation Fact-File
activities_projects.objective: Identify the system of production before the factory era
steps: Research the role of the Merchant in the countryside
Map the journey of wool: Stapler -> Spinner -> Weaver -> Fuller -> Dyer
Define why this period is called 'Proto' industrialization
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates between home-based production and factory production
activities_projects.evaluation: Correctness of the production sequence (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The First World War Economic Audit
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how war provided a boom for Indian industries
steps: Create a flow: British mills busy with war needs -> Imports to India decline -> Indian mills supply war needs (bags, uniforms) -> Boom in Indian industrial growth
Explain why Manchester could never recapture its market after the war
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects global geopolitical events to domestic industrial shifts
activities_projects.evaluation: Causal analysis and factual accuracy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Fly Shuttle Experiment
activities_projects.objective: Assess how technology helped small-scale weavers survive
steps: Research how the fly-shuttle increased the speed of weaving
Draw a diagram showing the difference between a traditional loom and a fly-shuttle loom
Explain how this helped handloom weavers compete with mill-made cloth
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understanding technological adaptations in small-scale industries

activities_projects.title: Urbanisation and its Discontents
activities_projects.objective: Examine the living conditions of the industrial poor
steps: Compare the housing of a worker in London with a mill worker in Mumbai (Chawls)
Write a report on the 'unplanned' nature of early industrial cities
Identify two health or social problems caused by overcrowding
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the social cost of rapid industrial growth
activities_projects.evaluation: Detail of sociological research and report quality (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Market for Goods Project
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the rise of consumer culture through advertisements
steps: Examine how calendars were used as a marketing tool in houses of both rich and poor
Explain the psychological impact of seeing 'Made in Manchester' alongside images of royalty
Draft a short pitch for a 'Swadeshi' product to compete with foreign goods
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesis of marketing, nationalism, and consumer behavior
